title: Microbiology & Infection Control

infection_chain.name: Infectious Agent
infection_chain.description: Pathogen capable of causing disease — bacteria, virus, fungus, parasite, prion.
infection_chain.intervention: Antimicrobial therapy when indicated; sterilization/disinfection; proper specimen handling
infection_chain.nursing_priority: Identify organism and susceptibility; apply standard precautions for all patients.

infection_chain.name: Reservoir
infection_chain.description: Environment where pathogen lives and multiplies — humans, animals, soil, water, fomites.
infection_chain.intervention: Environmental cleaning; food/water safety; source control of colonized patients
infection_chain.nursing_priority: Terminal cleaning of rooms; dedicated equipment for MDRO when policy requires.

infection_chain.name: Portal of Exit
infection_chain.description: Route pathogen leaves reservoir — respiratory secretions, blood, GI tract, skin lesions.
infection_chain.intervention: Respiratory hygiene; contained wound care; safe handling of body fluids
infection_chain.nursing_priority: Gloves/gown when contact with fluids expected; leak-proof waste disposal.

infection_chain.name: Mode of Transmission
infection_chain.description: Contact, droplet, airborne, vector, or vehicle spread from source to host.
infection_chain.intervention: Hand hygiene; transmission-based precautions; safe injection practices
infection_chain.nursing_priority: Match precautions to pathogen — contact for C. diff; airborne for TB.

infection_chain.name: Portal of Entry
infection_chain.description: Mucous membranes, respiratory tract, GI tract, broken skin, genitourinary tract.
infection_chain.intervention: Skin antisepsis; sterile technique; device bundles (CLABSI, CAUTI prevention)
infection_chain.nursing_priority: Minimize invasive devices; maintain closed drainage systems.

infection_chain.name: Susceptible Host
infection_chain.description: Person lacking immunity or with compromised defenses — age, illness, immunosuppression.
infection_chain.intervention: Vaccination; nutrition/rest; protective and neutropenic precautions when indicated
infection_chain.nursing_priority: Assess immune status; reverse isolation for neutropenia per protocol.

agent.label: Sterilization/disinfection of equipment
agent.explanation: Reduces viable pathogens on fomites and instruments.
agent.clinical_why: Targets the infectious agent directly on surfaces and devices.

agent.label: Appropriate antimicrobial therapy per culture
agent.explanation: Treats active infection caused by the pathogen.
agent.clinical_why: Stewardship: culture first when possible, shortest effective course.

agent.label: Vaccination of exposed staff
agent.explanation: Vaccination builds host immunity — it does not eliminate the agent link.
agent.clinical_why: Host susceptibility intervention, not agent destruction.

reservoir.label: Environmental cleaning (EPA-registered disinfectant)
reservoir.explanation: Removes pathogens from surfaces where they survive.
reservoir.clinical_why: C. diff requires bleach; C. auris needs EPA claim against organism.

reservoir.label: Patient isolation alone
reservoir.explanation: Isolation primarily breaks transmission, not the reservoir itself.
reservoir.clinical_why: Pair isolation with cleaning and source control.

portal_exit.label: Respiratory hygiene/cough etiquette
portal_exit.explanation: Contains pathogens leaving via respiratory tract.
portal_exit.clinical_why: Cover coughs; mask patient with respiratory infection.

portal_exit.label: Contained wound drainage and dressing changes
portal_exit.explanation: Prevents spread from portal of exit.
portal_exit.clinical_why: Standard precautions for all body fluids.

portal_exit.label: Skip hand hygiene after patient contact
portal_exit.explanation: Hand hygiene is essential after touching portals of exit.

transmission.label: Appropriate PPE per transmission-based precautions
transmission.clinical_why: N95 + contact for varicella until lesions crusted.

transmission.label: Reuse non-cleaned equipment room-to-room
transmission.clinical_why: Dedicated equipment or disinfection between uses.

portal_entry.label: Sterile technique for invasive devices
portal_entry.explanation: Protects mucous membranes and sterile sites.
portal_entry.clinical_why: Central line bundles prevent bloodstream infection.

portal_entry.label: Reuse needles between patients
portal_entry.explanation: Never reuse sharps — bloodborne pathogen risk.
portal_entry.clinical_why: Single-use sharps disposal in puncture-resistant container.

host.label: Vaccination per schedule
host.explanation: Builds adaptive immunity in susceptible host.

host.label: Nutrition, rest, and chronic disease management
host.clinical_why: Malnutrition and stress increase infection risk.

host.label: Routine prophylactic antibiotics for all patients
host.explanation: Contributes to resistance and CDI — only specific indications.
host.clinical_why: Surgical prophylaxis is time-limited per protocol.

microbe_classification.notes: Most treatable with antibiotics; Gram stain guides empiric therapy.
Require host cell; antivirals limited; vaccines prevent many.
Protozoa and helminths; travel and sanitation relevant.
No routine sterilization in community settings; CJD precautions for neuro tissue.

healthcare_pathogens.name: MRSA
healthcare_pathogens.nursing_action: Dedicated equipment; nasal decolonization per protocol; wound care with contact precautions
healthcare_pathogens.clinical_why: Colonization vs infection — contact precautions prevent spread in facilities.

healthcare_pathogens.name: C. diff
healthcare_pathogens.precautions: Contact precautions; soap-and-water hand hygiene (alcohol gel ineffective on spores)
healthcare_pathogens.nursing_action: Bleach environmental cleaning; discontinue inciting antibiotics per order; contact precautions
healthcare_pathogens.clinical_why: Antibiotic-associated diarrhea — toxin causes pseudomembranous colitis.

healthcare_pathogens.name: Influenza
healthcare_pathogens.nursing_action: Antivirals per order within 48 hr when indicated; droplet PPE; encourage vaccination
healthcare_pathogens.clinical_why: High mortality in elderly and immunocompromised.

healthcare_pathogens.name: TB
healthcare_pathogens.precautions: Airborne precautions; N95 respirator; negative-pressure AIIR
healthcare_pathogens.nursing_action: Place in AIIR; wear fit-tested N95; initiate airborne precautions until ruled out or noninfectious
healthcare_pathogens.clinical_why: Airborne spread via droplet nuclei — occupational health priority.

healthcare_pathogens.name: Norovirus
healthcare_pathogens.nursing_action: Contact precautions; environmental cleaning; restrict ill staff from work
healthcare_pathogens.clinical_why: Highly contagious gastroenteritis — outbreaks in facilities.

healthcare_pathogens.name: Pseudomonas
healthcare_pathogens.precautions: Standard precautions; contact if MDR/colonization per policy
healthcare_pathogens.nursing_action: VAP bundle: HOB 30–45°, oral care, closed suction; avoid moist reservoirs
healthcare_pathogens.clinical_why: Opportunist in moist environments and ventilated patients.

healthcare_pathogens.name: Candida auris
healthcare_pathogens.precautions: Contact precautions; notify infection control immediately
healthcare_pathogens.nursing_action: EPA-registered disinfectant with C. auris claim; cohorting/screening per protocol
healthcare_pathogens.clinical_why: Environmental persistence — many routine disinfectants ineffective.

healthcare_pathogens.name: Hepatitis B
healthcare_pathogens.nursing_action: Never recap needles; hepatitis B vaccination for healthcare workers
healthcare_pathogens.clinical_why: Percutaneous exposure risk with sharps injuries.

concepts.title: Standard Precautions
concepts.summary: Treat all blood/body fluids as potentially infectious
concepts.content: Hand hygiene, PPE when exposure anticipated, safe sharps handling, respiratory hygiene. Foundation for all patient care regardless of diagnosis.
concepts.clinical_relevance: Applied to every patient encounter — NCLEX infection control baseline.
nursing_implications: Perform hand hygiene before and after patient contact
Use gloves for potential fluid contact

concepts.title: Transmission-Based Precautions
concepts.summary: Contact, droplet, and airborne additions to standard precautions
concepts.content: Contact: MDRO, C. diff, norovirus. Droplet: influenza, pertussis (~3–6 ft). Airborne: TB, measles, varicella (requires airborne + contact until crusted).
concepts.clinical_relevance: Varicella needs N95/AIIR AND contact precautions.

concepts.title: Antimicrobial Stewardship
concepts.summary: Optimize antibiotic use to reduce resistance and CDI
concepts.content: Obtain cultures before antibiotics when possible. Use narrowest effective spectrum for shortest effective duration per guideline. Educate patients not to share or save antibiotics.
concepts.clinical_relevance: Overuse drives C. diff and MDRO emergence.
nursing_implications: Monitor for CDI with new diarrhea on antibiotics

concepts.title: Healthcare-Associated Infections (HAI)
concepts.summary: CLABSI, CAUTI, VAP, SSI, CDI prevention bundles
concepts.content: Central line bundle, urinary catheter stewardship, ventilator bundle, surgical antisepsis, and contact precautions for C. diff with soap-and-water hand hygiene.
concepts.clinical_relevance: Preventable harm — nursing bundles are first-line defense.

concepts.title: Medical vs Surgical Asepsis
concepts.content: Medical asepsis reduces microorganisms (hand hygiene, clean gloves). Surgical asepsis eliminates organisms in sterile field (OR, central line insertion).
nursing_implications: Clean vs sterile wound care per order

concepts.title: Immunization Principles
concepts.summary: Active and passive immunity in infection prevention
concepts.content: Vaccines stimulate antibody production (active). Immunoglobulins provide temporary passive immunity. Herd immunity protects vulnerable hosts.
concepts.clinical_relevance: Healthcare workers need hepatitis B, influenza, MMR, varicella per facility policy.
nursing_implications: Observe for 15 min post-vaccine if policy requires

concepts.title: Multidrug-Resistant Organisms (MDRO)
concepts.summary: MRSA, VRE, ESBL, CRE, C. diff, C. auris
concepts.content: Contact precautions, dedicated equipment, hand hygiene, and environmental cleaning. Antibiotic stewardship limits emergence.
concepts.clinical_relevance: MDRO spread is preventable with contact precautions and cleaning.

concepts.title: Specimen Collection & Chain of Custody
concepts.content: Collect specimens aseptically before starting empiric antibiotics when delay is safe. Label at bedside, transport per policy.
concepts.clinical_relevance: Contaminated specimens lead to inappropriate therapy.
nursing_implications: Use sterile technique for sterile sites

gram_stain_procedure: Fix smear with heat or methanol
Crystal violet (primary stain) — all cells purple
Iodine mordant — forms crystal violet-iodine complex
Alcohol decolorization — Gram- lose purple; Gram+ retain

gram_negative.cell_wall: Thin peptidoglycan + outer membrane with LPS

gram_stain_interpretation.clinical_note: Gram stain guides empiric antibiotics before culture sensitivities return.

soap_and_water_required: After caring for patients with infectious spores
hand_hygiene.alcohol_rub: Effective for most routine care when hands not visibly soiled — rub until dry.
hand_hygiene.nursing_priority: Single most effective action to prevent HAI.

ppe_guide.gloves: When contact with blood/body fluids, mucous membranes, non-intact skin anticipated
ppe_guide.mask: Droplet precautions; surgical mask within 3–6 ft
ppe_guide.sequence: Don: gown → mask/respirator → goggles → gloves. Doff inside room avoiding contamination.

break_chain_scenarios.title: MRSA Contact Precautions
break_chain_scenarios.scenario: New admission with MRSA in wound culture.
break_chain_scenarios.question: Which intervention best breaks transmission?
options: Gown and gloves on room entry
Alcohol gel only after room exit
No PPE if hands look clean
break_chain_scenarios.explanation: Contact precautions with gown/gloves block contact transmission.
break_chain_scenarios.clinical_why: Dedicated equipment and hand hygiene complete the bundle.

break_chain_scenarios.title: C. diff Outbreak
break_chain_scenarios.scenario: Three patients on unit develop watery diarrhea on antibiotics.
break_chain_scenarios.question: Priority environmental intervention?
break_chain_scenarios.explanation: C. diff spores require sporicidal cleaning.
break_chain_scenarios.clinical_why: Pair with soap-and-water hand hygiene and contact precautions.

break_chain_scenarios.title: Needlestick Injury
break_chain_scenarios.scenario: Nurse sustains percutaneous injury from used needle.
break_chain_scenarios.question: Immediate priority?
options: Wash area, report exposure, initiate PEP per protocol
break_chain_scenarios.explanation: Bloodborne pathogen exposure protocol starts immediately.

break_chain_scenarios.title: Neutropenic Patient
break_chain_scenarios.question: Which nursing measure protects susceptible host?
options: Protective precautions: cooked food, avoid ill visitors
break_chain_scenarios.explanation: Reverse/protective precautions reduce exposure to pathogens.
break_chain_scenarios.clinical_why: Low ANC — fever is emergency; no live plants.

break_chain_scenarios.title: TB Rule-Out
break_chain_scenarios.scenario: Patient with chronic cough, night sweats, positive quantiferon.
break_chain_scenarios.question: First placement action?
options: Private room with door closed; airborne precautions until ruled out
break_chain_scenarios.explanation: Suspected TB requires airborne isolation immediately.
break_chain_scenarios.clinical_why: N95 fit-tested respirator; AIIR when available.

what_if_scenarios.title: What if alcohol gel is used after C. diff care?
what_if_scenarios.scenario: Nurse uses alcohol hand rub leaving C. diff room.
what_if_scenarios.question: Expected outcome?
options: Spores remain viable on hands — transmission risk continues
what_if_scenarios.explanation: Alcohol does not kill C. diff spores.

what_if_scenarios.title: What if urinary catheter stays 10 days without indication?
what_if_scenarios.scenario: Indwelling catheter without daily necessity review.
what_if_scenarios.question: Primary HAI risk?
what_if_scenarios.explanation: Prolonged catheterization is leading CAUTI risk.
what_if_scenarios.clinical_why: Remove as soon as clinically appropriate.

what_if_scenarios.title: What if broad-spectrum antibiotics continue 14 days without indication?
what_if_scenarios.scenario: Patient improves but antibiotics not discontinued.
what_if_scenarios.question: Most likely complication?
what_if_scenarios.explanation: Disrupted microbiome allows C. diff overgrowth.

what_if_scenarios.title: What if ventilated patient supine 24 hours?
what_if_scenarios.question: Aspiration/VAP risk?
options: Increases — aspiration and VAP risk rise

break_points: Hand hygiene before and after every patient contact
Remove invasive devices as soon as clinically safe
Antimicrobial stewardship — culture before antibiotics when possible

practice_questions.question: A patient with C. difficile diarrhea requires which hand hygiene method?
options: No hand hygiene if gloves worn
practice_questions.explanation: Spores are not killed by alcohol gel — soap and water with friction removes spores.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Contact precautions plus soap-and-water is mandatory for C. diff.

practice_questions.question: Which precaution type is required for pulmonary tuberculosis?
practice_questions.explanation: TB spreads via droplet nuclei — N95 and AIIR required.
practice_questions.clinical_why: TB spreads via droplet nuclei — N95 and AIIR required.

practice_questions.question: The nurse breaks which chain link by performing hand hygiene before patient care?
practice_questions.explanation: Hand hygiene interrupts contact transmission of pathogens.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Hand hygiene interrupts contact transmission of pathogens.

practice_questions.question: Varicella (chickenpox) requires:
options: Both airborne and contact precautions until lesions crusted
practice_questions.explanation: CDC: airborne isolation plus contact until all lesions crusted.
practice_questions.clinical_why: CDC: airborne isolation plus contact until all lesions crusted.

practice_questions.question: Vaccination primarily targets which chain link?
practice_questions.explanation: Vaccines reduce host susceptibility by building immunity.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Vaccines reduce host susceptibility by building immunity.

practice_questions.question: First action when entering room of patient on contact precautions?
options: Perform hand hygiene and don gown/gloves
practice_questions.explanation: Don PPE on entry; doff inside room avoiding contamination.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Don PPE on entry; doff inside room avoiding contamination.

practice_questions.question: CLABSI prevention includes:
options: Central line bundle: hand hygiene, maximal barrier, chlorhexidine prep, review necessity
Prophylactic IV antibiotics for all lines

practice_questions.question: A nurse with influenza should:
options: Stay home until fever-free per policy; antivirals if indicated
Wear N95 and continue all assignments

practice_questions.question: Gram stain of Gram-negative organisms shows:
practice_questions.explanation: Gram- bacteria have thin peptidoglycan and lipopolysaccharide outer membrane — stain pink.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Gram- bacteria have thin peptidoglycan and lipopolysaccharide outer membrane — stain pink.

practice_questions.question: Antimicrobial stewardship prioritizes:
options: Prophylactic antibiotics for all surgical patients indefinitely

application_questions.question: You enter a room after C. diff care without soap-and-water hand hygiene. What is the risk?
options: No risk if gloves were worn
Spores may spread to next patient via contact
application_questions.explanation: Gloves do not replace hand hygiene; spores persist on hands.
application_questions.clinical_why: Gloves do not replace hand hygiene; spores persist on hands.

application_questions.question: Post-op patient develops fever and erythema at IV site with purulent drainage. Priority nursing action?
options: Apply warm compress and continue infusion
Stop infusion, notify provider, culture site per order
application_questions.explanation: Suspected line infection — stop infusion, notify, culture.
application_questions.clinical_why: Suspected line infection — stop infusion, notify, culture.

application_questions.question: Roommate of patient with new MRSA wound infection should:
options: Be screened/cohorted per policy; contact precautions for index patient
application_questions.explanation: Contact precautions for MRSA; screen contacts per facility policy.
application_questions.clinical_why: Contact precautions for MRSA; screen contacts per facility policy.

application_questions.question: During influenza season, unvaccinated nurse develops fever and myalgia. Best action?
options: Wear droplet PPE and continue assignments
Self-isolate and notify occupational health per policy
application_questions.explanation: Ill HCP should not care for high-risk patients.
application_questions.clinical_why: Ill HCP should not care for high-risk patients.

application_questions.question: Patient on ventilator — which intervention reduces VAP risk?
options: HOB 30–45°, oral care, subglottic suction per protocol
application_questions.explanation: Ventilator bundle elements reduce aspiration pneumonia.
application_questions.clinical_why: Ventilator bundle elements reduce aspiration pneumonia.
